capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Weather closure notice in North Team and Museum opening announcement in All Staff together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Museum opening announcement is still draft, change only that record to scheduled; otherwise make no change. Leave Weather closure notice and every similarly named record untouched. In the final response, use exactly three bullets: Weather closure notice, Museum opening announcement, and Result. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two message records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./messagingctl` executable, using Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./messagingctl --help` to learn its interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to message records: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing state, its audit or notification data, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

Search for the exact name `Weather closure notice` in the `North Team` channel and the exact name `Museum opening announcement` in the `All Staff` channel. Start these two independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one messaging operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell backgrounding, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either result is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval and write, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start those retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the messaging executable, again with exactly one messaging operation per call. Use only IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search result, or has no status, stop without changing messaging state. Base the condition only on the complete retrieved `Museum opening announcement` record, never on a search summary.

If and only if that retrieved record's status is exactly `draft`, perform one messaging operation that changes only its returned ID to `scheduled`; otherwise make no change. Never update `Weather closure notice`. Leave all same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-channel records untouched. Do not create, delete, cancel, archive, or notify anything, do not issue any other update, and do not write backing state or audit evidence directly. After the conditional branch, run `python3 .protected/verify.py`.

Your final response must contain exactly these three Markdown bullet lines and no preamble, heading, blank bullet, or closing text: `- Weather closure notice: <returned ID>; North Team; retrieved status <status>`, `- Museum opening announcement: <returned ID>; All Staff; retrieved status <status>; final status <status>`, and `- Result: <whether the conditional update occurred and whether verification passed>`. Substitute only facts returned by the executable and verifier.
